programmes.show_description: One of Us: It's Sir Humphrey in trouble for a change when it's revealed he wrongly cleared an MI5 agent accused of spying. Naturally, Jim Hacker is very sympathetic. Vintage laughs.
Cheltenham: Final episode of the domestic sitcom. Sam's visit to Cheltenham for lunch with Nanny Parker is a big hit, but things start to go downhill on the way back to London.
A Real Partnership: With a financial crisis looming just as MPs are due for a pay rise, Humphrey needs all his flair to dress up his claim and push it through. Smart political comedy.
A Victory for Democracy: Classic political sitcom. After a series of difficult encounters, Jim Hacker begins to wonder whether the government runs the Foreign Office, or vice versa.
The Bishop's Gambit: Classic comedy. Jim must recommend the appointment of bishops to the Queen, but is not keen on the two offered by the Church. Humphrey prefers a third candidate.

Shopping from Home.
The Fledgling: Drama, starring Ed Stoppard and Keeley Hawes. Diplomat Sir Hallam Holland and his wife Lady Agnes move into 165 Eaton Place and begin the search for new staff.
The Ladybird: The house celebrates when Lady Agnes discovers she is pregnant, while Lady Persie falls under the spell of Oswald Mosley and his British Union of Fascists.
The Cuckoo: Lady Persie pursues her political interests with little regard to her family, while Sir Hallam gets dragged into the crisis facing the British monarchy.

Featuring the Sweet Shop sketch. Join Ronnies Barker and Corbett for more sparkling comedy from the 1980s, featuring classics like Nothing's Too Much Trouble and Star Flaws.
Join the much-missed kings of comedy, Ronnie Barker and Ronnie Corbett, with a special Christmas programme of spoofs, sketches and songs from 1980.
Featuring the Crossed Lines sketch. Inspired farce from Barker and Corbett, including the graduates' river picnic and the men whose names are derived from where they were conceived.
Timeless comedy with the bifocal duo. Sketches include Pismonounciation, Short-sighted Daughter and Band of Slaves.

Arctic: Frozen Frontier: Steve and his team of explorers head to Greenland in the Arctic. Kayaking the world's largest fjord, they dodge giant icebergs and hungry polar bears.
Mexico: Maya Underworlds: Steve heads into Yucatan, Mexico, on the trail of the ancient Maya. Uncovering secrets and artefacts, they push far into the underworld of the Maya.
Arctic: Ice Mountain: Steve leads a team on a mission to climb a remote mountain in the Stauning Alps of Greenland, crossing a vast river and skiing across a glacier.
Mexico: Flooded Caves: Steve leads a team into the largest unexplored cave network on Earth. Below the jungles of Mexico, they push further and deeper than anyone has gone before.

Scorpion: Series two of the classic World War II series set in occupied Belgium. One of the 19 evaders in a Brussels safehouse is a German plant - but which one?
Weekend: Series two of the classic, meticulously-researched WWII drama set in occupied Belgium. Two American evaders decide to go it alone and take hostages.
